mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 5.0% signals tightening labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with UNRATE < 5.0% show negative or neutral performance (mean < 0.1% or t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates expanding demand conditions that elevate corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns show negative correlation with combined GDPC1/HOUST growth (Rank IC < -0.05) or individual series show no predictive power (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A 20-day decline in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) below 1.5% signals deflationary risks that prompt central bank intervention, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in CPIAUCSL < 1.5% periods show negative performance (mean < -0.2% or t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 0.5% signals strengthening manufacturing activity that boosts corporate margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in INDPRO > 0.5% periods show negative or neutral performance (mean < 0.1% or t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in consumer spending (RSAFS) and GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates weakening demand conditions that lower earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns show positive correlation with combined RSAFS/GDPC1 decline (Rank IC > 0.05) or individual series show no predictive power (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 200k signals strong labor market conditions that boost consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in PAYEMS > 200k periods show negative performance (mean < -0.2% or t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 0.2% signals weakening supply chain conditions that increase inflationary pressures, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in INDPRO < 0.2% periods show positive performance (mean > 0.1% or t-test p < 0.05).
